title: Open-String Quantum Lefschetz Formula
speaker: Renato Vianna
affiliation: Federal University of Rio de Janeiro
abstract: Let Y be a symplectic divisor of X, ω. In the Kahler setting, Givental's Quantum Lefschetz formula relates certain Gromov-Witten invariants (encoded by the G function) of X and Y. Given an Lagrangian L in (Y, ω|Y), we can lift it to a Lagrangian L' in neighbourhood NY ⊂X. We will introduce the notion of the potential of a Lagrangian, which encodes information of Maslov index 2 J-holomorphic disks with boundary on it. We will discuss the conditions in which the potential for L' relates with the potential for L according to a lifting formula. In particular, this formula involves counts J-holomorphic spheres with certain tangency on Y (part of relative Gromov-Witten invariants). It generalizes a formula that can be extracted from Biran-Khanevski, under some more restrictive assumptions on Y. As applications, we recover some Lefschetz formulas appearing in the work of Coates-Corti-Galkin-Kasprczyk and show the existence of infinitely many Lagrangian tori in CPn, Quadrics, Cubics, among other symplectic manifolds. This is joint work with Luis Diogo, Dmitry Tonkonog and Weiwei Wu.

proof_stages.label: Closed-to-open Lefschetz framework
proof_stages.summary: The talk frames quantum Lefschetz as relating descendant Gromov-Witten data of Y and X, then states the open-string goal of relating superpotentials for a Lagrangian in Y and its lift in X.

proof_stages.label: Biran lift and disk potentials
proof_stages.summary: For a Lagrangian L_Y in Y, the speaker defines the Biran lift L_X in the normal neighborhood, the group-ring superpotential counting Maslov-index-two disks, the uniquely lifted disk classes, and the fiber disk term.

proof_stages.label: General open-string quantum Lefschetz formula
proof_stages.summary: Under divisorial positivity and relative-position hypotheses, the theorem expresses W_{L_X} as the lifted W_{L_Y}, the fiber disk contribution, and a sum of correction terms identified with genus-zero relative Gromov-Witten counts of spheres in X tangent to Y.

proof_stages.label: Restricted invariance and divisor control
proof_stages.summary: Outside the monotone case the potential is not an unrestricted invariant, so the formula is interpreted for almost-complex structures preserving chosen base-point-free divisor systems, which control bubbling and allow invariance under restricted deformations.

proof_stages.label: Monotone-pair specialization
proof_stages.summary: In the monotone-pair setting the speaker recalls Biran-Khanevsky/Biran results giving monotone lifts and, when the minimal Chern number of Y is at least two, only lifted and fiber disk terms; the borderline Chern-one case yields a simplified formula with essentially one relative Gromov-Witten correction.

proof_stages.label: Reason for the stronger non-monotone hypotheses
proof_stages.summary: Results recovering closed G-series from monotone Lagrangian superpotentials and explicit algebraic quantum Lefschetz computations motivate replacing the monotone-pair assumptions by ample/base-point-free divisorial hypotheses with a relative-position condition on the Lagrangian.

proof_stages.label: Neck-stretching proof of the correction terms
proof_stages.summary: The proof sketches how local disks give the lifted and fiber terms, while neck stretching forces any additional curve to break into controlled outside levels whose tangency to Y and gluing multiplicities produce the relative Gromov-Witten and descendant coefficients.

proof_stages.label: Applications and limitations
proof_stages.summary: The formula is applied to distinguish lifted monotone Lagrangian tori, realize predicted superpotentials on Fano hypersurfaces, handle an example beyond monotone pairs, and the speaker notes that a general theory with Chern-zero sphere contributions is not yet known.
